tool.name: sdust
tool.description: mask low-complexity regions with symmetric DUST

param.name: input
param.label: Input FASTA
param.help: Nucleotide sequences, plain or gzipped. Low-complexity intervals are reported in BED.

param.name: window
param.label: Window size
param.help: Width of the sliding window scored for low complexity (sdust default 64).

param.name: threshold
param.label: Score threshold
param.help: Minimum DUST score for an interval to be masked (sdust default 20). Higher = stricter; fewer regions masked.

tool.help:
**sdust** — symmetric DUST low-complexity masker.
`sdust` scans nucleotide sequences and reports low-complexity intervals (homopolymer runs, short tandem repeats, etc.) using the symmetric DUST algorithm. Output is a 3-column BED of the masked intervals — one line per low-complexity region, in `name`, `start`, `end` (0-based, half-open) coordinates.
This is the masking step used in pangenome alignment pipelines to keep low-complexity sequence from generating spurious all-vs-all matches.
-----
**Inputs**
- `Input FASTA` — nucleotide sequences, plain or gzipped.
**Outputs**
- `low-complexity intervals (BED)` — 3-column BED (`seq`, `start`, `end`) of every masked region. Empty if no region scores above the threshold.
**Options**
- `-w` window size (default 64). - `-t` score threshold (default 20); raise it to mask less.
-----
sdust is distributed with the minimap2 / mappy toolkit and implements the symmetric DUST scheme of Morgulis *et al.* (2006).
